sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 11.116 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.11)

statement.latex: The dimension\/ of a partially ordered set$< P, \, >$is, by definition, the least cardinal number$$such that the relation$$is an intersection of$$relations of linear order on$P$. Is it true that, for any Chernikov group which does not contain a direct product of two quasicyclic groups over the same prime number, the subgroup lattice has finite dimension? Expected answer:\/ yes.

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
